title: Purnima Devi Barman Named in TIME's 2025 Women of the Year
what_talking_about: Dr. Purnima Devi Barman received recognition from TIME magazine for conserving the greater adjutant stork in Assam, lifting the species from endangered to near-threatened status.
Her 'Hargila Army' of 20,000+ women protects nesting sites and educates communities, blending conservation with women's economic empowerment.
why_care: Her work demonstrates how local action sparks global change while protecting vulnerable populations.
It's a rare story of a conservation effort that is led by women, empowers women economically, and has measurably improved the status of an endangered species.

sources.title: TIME: This Conservationist Is Saving One of the World's Most Endangered Storks
